todaysEvents.summary: Trump extends ceasefire indefinitely at Pakistan's request (Field Marshal Munir + PM Sharif); Iranian government described as 'seriously fractured'; US naval blockade remains; VP Vance Islamabad trip cancelled; 2nd round talks called off (CNBC, Axios, CBS, NPR, Apr 21).
todaysEvents.delta: De-escalation: ceasefire extended averts Apr 22 expiry tail scenario; indefinite extension buys diplomatic time

todaysEvents.summary: Iran: ceasefire extension 'has no meaning' (national security adviser); FM Araghchi: US blockade is 'act of war' and violates ceasefire terms; no unified Iranian proposal forthcoming; Touska retaliation vow stands (CBS, CNN, Apr 21-22).
todaysEvents.delta: Residual escalation: Iran rejects extension logic; blockade-as-ceasefire-violation creates new legal flashpoint; Touska retaliation threat unresolved

todaysEvents.summary: Brent ~$94 est. (-5.5%; last confirmed $95.42 TradingEconomics Apr 20); iTraxx Asia IG ~190bp est. (-15bp); ASEAN HY ~560bp est. (-25bp); tail risk drops to 33% on ceasefire extension; stress dominant at 52% (Apr 22).
todaysEvents.delta: Markets reprice on extension relief: energy and credit spreads ease; stress scenario becomes dominant; tail remains elevated at 33% on unresolved Iran retaliation threat

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: Extension creates narrow deal window — Iran's 'seriously fractured' government (per Trump) may allow Pakistani mediators to assemble a unified proposal. Requires Iran to stand down Touska retaliation + resume formal talks + accept nuclear commitment framework. Brent retreats to $85-92/bbl; iTraxx Asia IG tightens below 175bp. Conditional on Iranian government consolidating around a deal position — FM Araghchi calling blockade an 'act of war' signals continued resistance, but Pakistani channel still open.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: Dominant scenario — ceasefire extends indefinitely; blockade remains; Iran calls extension 'meaningless' but does not execute Touska retaliation. IRGC maintains Hormuz harassment posture without full closure. US-Pakistan-Iran back-channel produces no formal deal framework. Brent stabilises $90-108/bbl; JKM $18-22/MMBtu; iTraxx Asia IG 180-210bp. Stalemate extended, not resolved — both sides preserve optionality while avoiding kinetic escalation.

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: Iran executes Touska retaliation strike despite ceasefire extension — national security adviser's 'extension has no meaning' signals potential for unilateral action. FM Araghchi's 'act of war' framing creates legal pretext. IRGC targets US naval asset; ceasefire collapses; air campaign resumes. Kharg Island seizure or Aramco strike brings GCC production under acute threat. Brent spikes $130-170/bbl; LNG markets seize; ASEAN recession risk acute. Probability reduced from 50% (D50) on ceasefire extension but residual tail risk from unresolved Touska retaliation remains high.
